alignments.preprint_claim_text: REMS is the most responsive of all the measured sleep characteristics to chronic stress. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: REMS correlates with deficiency in corticosterone regulation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Apoptotic pathways, including mitochondrial pathways, are identified as a putative mechanism by which REMS mediates adaptation to stressful waking experiences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transcriptomic predictor sets for REMS and anhedonia share pathways involved in oxidative stress, cell proliferation, and apoptosis. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: REMS theta oscillations were enhanced, whereas delta oscillations in non-REMS were unaffected by UCMS. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: These data identify REMS as a core and early element of the response to chronic stress. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: mTBI patients showed decreased fractional anisotropy (FA) and increased mean diffusivity (MD) on DTI mainly in anterior tracts, signifying vasogenic edema. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early decreases of fractional anisotropy and increases of mean diffusivity corresponded to WM regions with elevated free water fraction on NODDI. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Longer-term longitudinal WM changes consisted of declining neurite density on NODDI, suggesting axonal degeneration from diffuse axonal injury. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NODDI parameters appear to be more sensitive to longitudinal changes than DTI metrics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NODDI is a more sensitive and specific biomarker than DTI for WM microstructural changes due to mTBI. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: White matter FA and MD alterations early after mTBI might reflect vasogenic edema, as shown by elevated free water on NODDI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early decreases of fractional anisotropy and increases of mean diffusivity corresponded to WM regions with elevated free water fraction on NODDI. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A collective feature selection approach can help in selecting true positive epistatic variables more frequently than any single method for feature selection. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study constructed the first AOP network relevant for inorganic cadmium-induced toxicity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Case studies were performed to verify the rationality of cadmium-induced toxicity in humans and aquatic species. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of shear stress and blood pressure with vessel radius was tightly correlated to vessel pruning sites. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: eaDCA demonstrates efficiency in generating artificial RNA sequences that closely resemble their natural counterparts in both statistical analyses and SHAPE-MaP experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: eaDCA demonstrates efficiency in generating artificial RNA sequences that closely resemble their natural counterparts in both statistical analyses and SHAPE-MaP experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: eaDCA suggests the existence of approximately 10^39 functional nucleotide sequences in the case of cyclic di-AMP riboswitches (RF00379). (certainty=low, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: eaDCA suggests the existence of approximately 10^39 functional nucleotide sequences in the case of cyclic di-AMP riboswitches (RF00379). (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Numeric estimate and caveated confidence are unchanged; classification type differs slightly but claim content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of potential functional sequences estimated by eaDCA represents only a tiny fraction of the vast pool of nearly 10^82 possible nucleotide sequences of the same length. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of potential functional sequences estimated by eaDCA represents only a tiny fraction of the vast pool of nearly 10^82 possible nucleotide sequences of the same length. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion in both documents with same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model proposes that the Posterior Parietal Cortex (PPC) maintains constantly updated expectations over the environmental state to generate goal-directed actions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural circuitry in the Posterior Parietal Cortex (PPC) computes flexible intentions from a belief over targets to dynamically generate goal-directed actions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes expectations as 'belief over targets' and emphasizes computation of intentions, clarifying the mechanism without changing scope or strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Active Inference driven by dynamic and flexible intentions can support goal-directed behavior in constantly changing environments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Active Inference driven by dynamic and flexible intentions can support goal-directed behavior in constantly changing environments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical in substance and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proof-of-concept agent successfully performed target-reaching tasks under various conditions, demonstrating the model's applicability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proof-of-concept agent behaved correctly under various conditions, including static and dynamic targets, different sensory feedbacks, sensory precisions, intention gains, and movement policies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication provides more detailed enumeration of tested conditions but preserves the original result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a normative basis for research on goal-directed behavior in end-to-end settings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a normative computational basis for research on goal-directed behavior in end-to-end settings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies 'computational' but retains the original contribution claim; this is a clarification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The PPC might putatively host the core intention mechanism for goal-directed actions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This speculative attribution of the core mechanism to PPC appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dichotomized Alda score is more robust to inter-rater disagreement than the raw 0-10 scale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dichotomized Alda score is more robust to inter-rater disagreement than the raw 0-10 scale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dichotomization of a continuous variable may be more informative concerning its ground truth value when a measure’s reliability is stronger at one extreme of the continuum. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dichotomization of a continuous variable may be more informative concerning its ground truth value when a measure's reliability is stronger at one extreme of the continuum. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Substantively identical conclusion with only minor formatting differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Research employing the Alda score of lithium responsiveness should continue using the dichotomous definition, particularly when data are sampled across multiple raters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Research employing the Alda score of lithium responsiveness should continue using the dichotomous definition, particularly when data are sampled across multiple raters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Recommendation is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep generative model successfully distinguishes cell-containing from cell-free droplets without supervision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep generative model accurately distinguishes cell-containing droplets from cell-free droplets.
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively the same claim about accurate separation of cell-containing vs cell-free droplets; meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellBender operates close to the theoretically optimal denoising limit in simulated datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of simulated data demonstrates that CellBender operates near the theoretically optimal denoising limit.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same result with equivalent emphasis and certainty about performance on simulated data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CellBender significantly improves the agreement of droplet-based single-cell data with established gene expression patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive evaluations using real datasets highlight enhanced concordance between droplet-based single-cell data and established gene expression patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the claim as results of extensive real-data evaluations (clarifying evidence) and downgrades certainty from 'high' to 'medium'.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned background noise profile provides evidence for degraded or uncaptured cell types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned background noise profile provides evidence of degraded or uncaptured cell types.
alignments.rationale: Text is essentially identical; claim content and confidence remain the same.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CellBender provides noise-free quantification in an end-to-end fashion.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about end-to-end noise-free quantification does not have a counterpart in the preprint and appears as an added assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The volatile Kalman filter (VKF) extends the Kalman filter algorithm to volatile environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The volatile Kalman filter (VKF) extends the Kalman filter algorithm to volatile environments.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VKF learns volatility according to a second error-correcting rule in addition to the error-correcting rule of the Kalman filter.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VKF learns volatility according to a second error-correcting rule in addition to the error-correcting rule of the Kalman filter.
alignments.rationale: Same technical mechanism described in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When fit to empirical data, the VKF better captures human choice data in two independent datasets of probabilistic learning tasks compared to existing models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When fit to empirical data, the VKF better captures human choice data in two independent datasets of probabilistic learning tasks compared to existing models.
alignments.rationale: Result is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model provides a coherent account of learning in stable or volatile environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model provides a coherent account of learning in stable or volatile environments.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion appears unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EECoG-Comp is an open source platform that encompasses preprocessing, forward modeling, simulation, and comparison modules for ESI methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the platform-as-open-source contribution; no matching publication claim is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of four popular ESI methods, namely MNE, LCMV, eLORETA, and SSBL, was compared using a simultaneous EECoG dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four popular ESI methods showed significant but moderate concordance with the ECoG Laplacian in real MDR data.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a specific comparative outcome (moderate concordance) that clarifies the preprint's statement about comparing methods on real simultaneous data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis demonstrates the limits of performance of ESI connectivity estimators using both simulations and real data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All ESI methods and the Laplacian had a significant but poor correspondence with the true source connectivity in both monkey and human simulations.
alignments.rationale: Publication gives concrete simulation results (poor correspondence) that specify the limits described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest the need for improved simultaneous EEG and ECoG experiments and ESI connectivity estimators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of improved ESI connectivity estimators may be stimulated by these preliminary results.
alignments.rationale: Both statements recommend development of improved estimators; the publication frames it as likely stimulus for further development.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The lack of specialized procedures for EECoG data mining hinders the mining of this type of data.
alignments.rationale: This limitation about data-mining procedures is present in the publication but not in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method developed in this study successfully identified more than 75% of large eels using datasets from ARIS and BlueView cameras.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method developed in this study successfully identified more than 75% of large eels using datasets from ARIS and BlueView cameras.
alignments.rationale: Result is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 42% of eels shorter than 60 cm were detected by the method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only 42% of eels shorter than 60 cm were detected by the method.
alignments.rationale: Identical performance result in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model performances for detecting eels were observed for detection ranges of 4-9 m.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model performances for detecting eels were observed for detection ranges of 4-9 m.
alignments.rationale: Same specific performance detail in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improvements are required to compensate for fish-length limitations in the detection method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improvements are required to compensate for fish-length limitations in the detection method.
alignments.rationale: Limitation unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cross-camera method is promising for automatically detecting and counting large eels in long-term monitoring studies in complex environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cross-camera method is promising for automatically detecting and counting large eels in long-term monitoring studies in complex environments.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about promise for monitoring is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework demonstrates that multi-modal fingerprints are more discriminative than those generated from individual modalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework demonstrates that multi-modal fingerprints are more discriminative than those generated from individual modalities.
alignments.rationale: Core result is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments indicate a link between fingerprint similarity and genetic proximity, with monozygotic twins having more similar fingerprints than dizygotic or non-twin siblings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments indicate a link between fingerprint similarity and genetic proximity, with monozygotic twins having more similar fingerprints than dizygotic or non-twin siblings.
alignments.rationale: Empirical finding is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness of the proposed framework to factors like image alignment and scan resolution suggests its potential for characterizing individuals in large cohort analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The robustness of the proposed framework to factors like image alignment and scan resolution suggests its potential for characterizing individuals in a large cohort analysis.
alignments.rationale: Robustness and potential for cohort analysis are retained with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed rank retrieval evaluation can be used for a standardized comparison of future brain fingerprints.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a corresponding claim about the rank retrieval evaluation as a standardized comparison tool.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The differences in feature correspondences between twin/sibling pairs occur in major brain structures and across hemispheres.
alignments.rationale: This specific anatomical localization of differences is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint, so it is an added, more detailed result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There were 213 differentially expressed proteins (DEPs) identified between the poor and higher cognition groups, with 155 DEPs being upregulated in the poor cognition group.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study discovered 213 differentially expressed proteins (DEPs) between lower and higher cognition groups, with 155 upregulated in the lower cognition group.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative result with synonymous wording (lower vs poor cognition).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified DEPs were significantly enriched in the IL-17 signaling pathway.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified DEPs were enriched in the IL-17 signaling pathway and linked to neuroinflammation-related cognitive impairment.
alignments.rationale: The core finding of IL-17 enrichment is preserved, but the publication adds an explicit link to neuroinflammation-related cognitive impairment, broadening implications and strengthening interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest model achieved a test classification accuracy of 81.5%, with a sensitivity of 65% and specificity of 85.9%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A random forest model classified cognitive ability groups with an accuracy of 81.5%, sensitivity of 65%, specificity of 85.9%, and an AUC of 0.79.
alignments.rationale: The numerical classification performance is the same; the publication adds an AUC value, which clarifies model performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggested that a measure of poor WCST performance in healthy Thai subjects might be attributed to higher levels of neuroinflammation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research provides novel insights into the link between neuroinflammation and cognitive performance, advancing our understanding of cognitive variability.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's tentative, population-specific attribution into a broader statement about links between neuroinflammation and cognitive performance and frames it more positively (higher certainty).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the role of biological markers in cognitive function and contributes to developing more accurate machine learning models for diverse populations.
alignments.rationale: This contribution about biomarkers and ML model development is new in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human gut microbial enzymes are capable of cleaving carbon-fluorine bonds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gut microbial enzymes can break carbon-fluorine bonds, potentially altering the toxicity of fluorinated compounds.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core result that gut enzymes cleave C–F bonds but adds an implication about possible effects on toxicity, broadening impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A rapid, miniaturized fluoride detection assay for whole-cell screening was developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Active gut microbial defluorinases were identified through a rapid, miniaturized fluoride detection assay.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's methodological claim is present; the publication emphasizes that the assay was used successfully to identify active enzymes, showing application of the method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models identified protein termini as important features for predicting defluorination activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models trained on protein C termini segments predicted defluorination activity with 83% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: Both claims focus on the importance of protein termini for prediction; the publication quantifies performance (accuracy), clarifying model effectiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Flexible C-terminal loop residues were predictive of defluorination activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models trained on protein C termini segments predicted defluorination activity with 83% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific observation about C-terminal loop residues aligns with the publication's broader C-termini-based predictive models, though the publication frames it in model-performance terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work supports using convergent structural features to predict defluorination specificity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models trained on protein C termini segments predicted defluorination activity with 83% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication's model-result supports the preprint's conclusion about using structural features (C-termini) to predict defluorination specificity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study biochemically characterized enzymes from diverse human gut microbial classes that can hydrolyze (di)fluorinated organic acids and a fluorinated amino acid.
alignments.rationale: The biochemical characterization of diverse enzymes and substrates appears as a new, detailed result in the publication not described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The INFLAMeR model successfully reconstituted CRISPRi screening data and predicted functional lncRNAs that were previously overlooked.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The INFLAMeR model successfully reconstituted CRISPRi screening data and predicted functional lncRNAs that were previously overlooked.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim about INFLAMeR's performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Intensive cell-based and transcriptomic validation of nearly fifty genes in K562 revealed cell type-specific functionality for 85% of the predicted lncRNAs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Intensive cell-based and transcriptomic validation of nearly fifty genes in K562 revealed cell type-specific functionality for 85% of the predicted lncRNAs.
alignments.rationale: The validation result and percentage are restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Knockdown of SNHG6 reduced the proliferation of leukemia cells and sensitized them to differentiation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SNHG6 knockdown reduced the proliferation of leukemia cells and sensitized them to differentiation.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SNHG6 inhibits red blood cell differentiation but strongly promotes megakaryocyte differentiation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SNHG6 inhibits red blood cell differentiation but strongly promotes megakaryocyte differentiation.
alignments.rationale: The biological directionality is identical, but the publication lists a lower certainty for this claim (medium vs high), indicating a tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach improved the identification and characterization of functional lncRNAs through genomic approaches in a cell type-specific manner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The INFLAMeR model successfully reconstituted CRISPRi screening data and predicted functional lncRNAs that were previously overlooked.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents the model's success which supports the preprint's methodological conclusion; the publication reports specific results rather than the broader methodological claim, so mapping is moderately confident.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SNHG6 knockdown attenuated tumor growth in a xenograft mouse model.
alignments.rationale: This in vivo xenograft result is new in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Behavioral flow analysis (BFA) creates a single metric for all observed transitions between behavioral motifs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced pipeline captures each animal’s behavioral flow, yielding a single metric based on all observed transitions between clusters.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution of producing a single metric from behavioral transitions with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier-in-the-middle approach stabilizes clusters and enables transferability of analyses across datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline stabilizes clusters through machine learning, ensuring data transferability across experiments.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same methodological effect (cluster stabilization and transferability) using synonymous descriptions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis pipeline increases statistical power compared to conventional techniques and has strong reproducibility across experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline enhances statistical power and enables predictions of an animal’s future behavior.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms enhanced statistical power and emphasizes predictive ability; reproducibility is less explicitly stated, so the claim is somewhat clarified in emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficient individual phenotyping allows classification of stress-responsiveness and prediction of future behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline enhances statistical power and enables predictions of an animal’s future behavior.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the pipeline's predictive capability, which matches the preprint's claim about predicting future behavior.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach aligns with animal welfare regulations by reducing animal numbers and enhancing information extracted from experimental animals.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize the animal-welfare claim; this conclusion appears absent from the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset of 771 behavior recordings allows for the identification of hidden treatment effects and reveals subtle variations at the individual animal level.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a new dataset-scale result (771 recordings) and its consequences that were not specified in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The freshwater community assemblage and functionality changed over time without returning to its original state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The freshwater community assemblage and functionality changed over time without returning to its original state, even if the lake partially recovered in recent times.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core result but adds nuance about a partial recent recovery; the main claim (no full return to original state) remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Insecticides and fungicides, combined with extreme temperature events and precipitation, explained up to 90% of the functional biodiversity changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Insecticides and fungicides, combined with extreme temperature events and precipitation, explained up to 90% of the functional biodiversity changes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative attribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The community-level biodiversity approach used here reliably explained freshwater ecosystem shifts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The community-level biodiversity approach used here reliably explained freshwater ecosystem shifts, which were not observed when using traditional quality indices.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the original claim and adds that these shifts were not detected by traditional indices, clarifying the method's advantage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shifts in biodiversity were not observed when using traditional quality indices like the Trophic Diatom Index.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The community-level biodiversity approach used here reliably explained freshwater ecosystem shifts, which were not observed when using traditional quality indices.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges this observation into a combined claim about the method detecting shifts that traditional indices miss; the content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study advocates the use of high throughput systemic approaches on long-term trends over species-focused ecological surveys to identify environmental factors causing loss of biodiversity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of environmental DNA from sediment can provide rich historical biodiversity data, which can be used to train artificial intelligence-based models to improve predictions of how ecosystems will respond to complex environmental changes.
alignments.rationale: The publication concretely articulates one high-throughput approach (eDNA + AI) consistent with the preprint's advocacy, expanding and specifying the recommendation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings can be used by environmental regulators to identify species or ecosystems at risk from environmental change and prioritize them for intervention.
alignments.rationale: This regulatory-application framing is new in the publication and was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings reveal increased hierarchical organization at the whole-brain level and within specific resting-state networks in individuals with schizophrenia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings reveal increased hierarchical organisation at the whole-brain level and within specific resting-state networks in individuals with schizophrenia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in content and certainty to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased hierarchical organization correlated with negative symptoms, positive formal thought disorder and apathy in individuals with schizophrenia. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased hierarchical organisation correlated with negative symptoms, positive formal thought disorder and apathy in individuals with schizophrenia. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The correlation findings and level of certainty are the same in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We showed that hierarchy measures allow a robust diagnostic separation between healthy controls and schizophrenia patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We showed that hierarchy measures allow a robust diagnostic separation between healthy controls and schizophrenia patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The diagnostic separation claim is carried over verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings provide new insights into the nature of functional connectivity anomalies in schizophrenia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings provide new insights into the nature of functional connectivity anomalies in schizophrenia. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about new insights is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The breakdown of the functional orchestration of brain dynamics could be a cause of functional connectivity anomalies in schizophrenia. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The breakdown of the functional orchestration of brain dynamics could be a cause of functional connectivity anomalies in schizophrenia. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The speculative causal statement is unchanged in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NGN2 and MyoD1, despite recognizing the same E-box motifs, instigate distinct cell fates—neurons and muscles, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurogenin-2 (NGN2) and MyoD1 recognize ubiquitous E-box motifs but drive distinct cell fates toward neurons and muscles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published phrasing restates the same result (same factors, same distinct fates) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reveals a chromatin-dependent motif syntax that delineates both common and factor-specific binding of transcription factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A chromatin-dependent motif syntax delineates both common and factor-specific binding of transcription factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim about chromatin-dependent motif syntax is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Factor-specific binding in closed chromatin involves NGN2 and MyoD1 acting as pioneer-factors, influenced by various factors including multi-motifs and specific interaction partners. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Factor-specific binding in closed chromatin involves NGN2 and MyoD1 acting as pioneer factors influenced by motif variant frequencies and spacing. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim refines the mechanistic contributors (specifying motif variant frequencies and spacing) compared with the broader list in the preprint, so the content is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology extends to other models, demonstrating that opportunistic-binding and context-specific chromatin opening underpin transcription factor specificity driving differentiation trajectories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A combination of opportunistic binding and context-specific chromatin-opening underpins transcription factor specificity, driving differentiation trajectories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about opportunistic binding and context-specific opening underpinning specificity is preserved with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WarpDemuX is an ultra-fast and highly accurate adapter-barcoding and demultiplexing approach for nanopore direct RNA sequencing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WarpDemuX is an ultra-fast and highly accurate adapter-barcoding and demultiplexing approach for dRNA-seq. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same; wording is abbreviated but the meaning and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WarpDemuX enhances speed and accuracy by fast processing of the raw nanopore signal and the use of a light-weight machine-learning algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WarpDemuX enhances speed and accuracy by fast processing of the raw nanopore signal, use of a light-weight machine-learning algorithm, and design of optimized barcode sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim adds a specific design element (optimized barcode sets) to the mechanisms explaining improved performance, clarifying the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of WarpDemuX allows for rapid phenotypic profiling of different SARS-CoV-2 viruses through multiplexed sequencing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WarpDemuX enables rapid phenotypic profiling of different SARS-CoV-2 viruses through multiplexed sequencing of longitudinal samples on a single flowcell. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim specifies the experimental context (longitudinal samples on a single flowcell), adding detail to the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WarpDemuX identifies systematic differences in transcript abundance and poly(A) tail lengths during SARS-CoV-2 infection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific result about transcript abundance and poly(A) tail length differences is not represented among the publication's listed claims and therefore appears removed from the main claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating WarpDemuX into sequencing control software enables real-time enrichment of target molecules through barcode-specific adaptive sampling. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating WarpDemuX into sequencing control software enables real-time enrichment of target molecules through barcode-specific adaptive sampling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the published claim elevates the certainty/type (from a medium-confidence conclusion to a high-confidence result), reflecting amplified confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: WarpDemuX represents a broadly applicable, high-performance, economical multiplexing solution for dRNA-seq. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a broad concluding claim about general applicability and economy that was not present in the preprint claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MEA-seqX platform enables simultaneous recording and analysis of molecular and electrical network activities at the level of individual cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MEA-seqX platform enables the simultaneous recording and analysis of molecular and electrical network activities at mesoscale spatial resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claim changes the asserted spatial scale from 'individual cells' to 'mesoscale spatial resolution', which revises the claimed resolution (a narrower/smaller-scale claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MEA-seqX unveiled massively enhanced nested dynamics between transcription and function in a mouse hippocampal model of experience-dependent plasticity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MEA-seqX unveils massively enhanced nested dynamics between transcription and function in a mouse hippocampal model of experience-dependent plasticity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about nested dynamics is preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Graph-theoretic analysis revealed an increase in densely connected bimodal hubs, marking the first observation of coordinated spatiotemporal dynamics in hippocampal circuitry at both molecular and functional levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Graph-theoretic analysis reveals an increase in densely connected bimodal hubs, marking the first observation of coordinated hippocampal circuitry dynamics at molecular and functional levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The graph-theoretic observation is carried over with equivalent meaning and certainty; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-learning algorithms accurately predicted network-wide electrophysiological features from spatial gene expression. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Genetically manipulating the Akh pathway can rescue Tra-dependent effects on fat storage. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A genome-wide association study (GWAS) identified 277 unique associations related to adventitious rooting in Populus trichocarpa. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A genome-wide association study identified 277 unique associations related to adventitious rooting in Populus trichocarpa. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified genes that control rooting include regulators of hormone signaling, cell division and structure, and reactive oxygen species signaling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified genes that control rooting include regulators of hormone signaling, cell division and structure, and reactive oxygen species signaling. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study developed a computer vision system to measure traits related to adventitious root development in poplar. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about a computer vision system present in the preprint; this contribution appears omitted from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerous genes with uncharacterized functions and/or cryptic roles were identified as candidates for further functional analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The identification of uncharacterized candidate genes is present in both versions with equivalent content; only the framing (result→contribution) differs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tasselyzer is a novel image-based segmentation tool for automated, large-scale measurement of anther exertion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tasselyzer is an accessible, cost-effective, and time-saving method for quantifying maize anther exertion.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the tool emphasizing accessibility, cost- and time-savings (a clarification and stronger emphasis on benefits) while preserving the core claim that it quantifies anther exertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated method developed provides a quantitative assessment of anther exertion, capturing regional differences within the tassel.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The image-based program Tasselyzer provides a quantitative assessment of anther exertion by capturing regional differences within the tassel.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with effectively identical wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of Tasselyzer to diverse maize lines demonstrates its utility for research and breeding programs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tasselyzer is a valuable resource for maize research and breeding programs, enabling automated and efficient assessments of anther exertion.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert usefulness for research and breeding; the publication frames this as a value statement matching the preprint's asserted utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantifying anther exertion is a key indicator of male fertility in maize.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not reiterate this interpretive statement about male fertility, so the explicit claim is absent in the final claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of Tasselyzer to 22 maize lines with six genotypes showed high precision with an F<sub>1</sub> score greater than 0.8.
alignments.rationale: This specific performance result (22 lines, F1>0.8) is a new, more detailed quantitative result in the publication not present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Customizing the parameters of Tasselyzer to assay a specific line is straightforward and practical for enhancing precision in additional genotypes.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a practical-methods claim about parameter customization that was not stated as a separate claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed massively parallel NeRF implementation achieves speedups between 400-1200x over the previous state-of-the-art, depending on the polymer length.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed massively parallel NeRF implementation achieves speedups between 400 and 1200× over the previous state-of-the-art.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative speedup claim with essentially identical phrasing and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conversion process is divided into three main phases: parallel composition of the monomer backbone, assembly of backbone subunits, and parallel elongation of sidechains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conversion process is divided into three main phases: parallel composition of the monomer backbone, assembly of backbone subunits, and parallel elongation of sidechains.
alignments.rationale: The phase-wise description is carried over verbatim to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation emphasizes reusability and ease of use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation emphasizes reusability and ease of use.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same emphasis on reusability and ease-of-use.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The code and a corresponding python package are open-sourced and available at https://github.com/EleutherAI/mp_nerf.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The code and a corresponding python package are open sourced and available online.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that code and a package are openly available, with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel platform allows for the continuous and accurate quantification of complex behavioral features in Daphnia magna.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel platform allows for the continuous quantification of complex behavioral features in Daphnia magna.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the continuous quantification claim but drops the explicit word "accurate," reducing stated confidence in measurement accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive machine learning models can be built using chronological age to extrapolate onto phenotypic age.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive machine-learning models can be built using chronological age to extrapolate onto phenotypic age.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the modeling capability with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform can estimate phenotypic age under pharmacological perturbation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform can estimate phenotypic age under pharmacological perturbation.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses this claim with higher certainty (publication lists it as high certainty), increasing confidence relative to the preprint's medium certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform provides a scalable framework for drug screening and characterization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform provides a scalable framework for drug screening and characterization.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dysgu outperforms existing state-of-the-art tools using paired-end or long-reads.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dysgu outperforms existing state-of-the-art tools using paired-end or long-reads.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance superiority claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dysgu offers high sensitivity and precision whilst being among the fastest tools to run.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dysgu offers high sensitivity and precision whilst being among the fastest tools to run.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this performance and speed characterization exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining low coverage paired-end and long-reads is competitive in terms of performance with long-reads at higher coverage values.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining low coverage paired-end and long-reads is competitive in terms of performance with long-reads at higher coverage values.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about combined data sources remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inferred population structure from admixture and clustering analysis illustrated strong evidence of substructure in each geographical region.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inferred population structure from admixture and clustering analysis illustrated strong evidence of substructure in each geographical region.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of phylogenetic tree, PCA analysis, and machine learning on population identifying SNPs supported the inferred population structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of phylogenetic tree, PCA analysis, and machine learning on population identifying SNPs also supported the inferred population structure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The population structure inferred in this study contains five subpopulations that tend to group individuals based on location.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The population structure inferred in this study contains five subpopulations that tend to group individuals based on location.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about five location-associated subpopulations is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each subpopulation has unique genetic patterns, agronomic traits, as well as different environmental conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each subpopulation has unique genetic patterns, agronomic traits, as well as different environmental conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this interpretive claim about subpopulation differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study can serve as a reference point of the nation-wide population structure for supporting breeders and researchers who are interested in Thai rice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study can serve as a reference point of the nation-wide population structure for supporting breeders and researchers who are interested in Thai rice.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this stated contribution to breeders and researchers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Holographic cytometry allows for high quality phase imaging of a large number of cells, enhancing the study of cellular phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Holographic cytometry allows high-throughput phase imaging of a large number of cells, greatly extending our ability to study cellular phenotypes using individual cell images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Holographic cytology measurements show that multiple physical traits of red blood cells, including optical volume and area, consistently change over storage time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Measurements of multiple physical traits of red blood cells, including optical volume and area, consistently change over the storage time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about trait changes over storage time with the same specifics and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The large volume of cell imaging data from holographic cytometry can serve as training data for machine learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The large volume of cell imaging data can serve as training data for machine-learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Logistic regression can be used to classify red blood cells according to their storage time points. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the classifiers demonstrate the potential of holographic cytometry as a diagnostic tool. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Overall, results showed the potential of holographic cytometry as a diagnostic tool. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DAnIEL web server is dedicated to the comprehensive analysis of the human mycobiome. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DAnIEL web server is dedicated to the comprehensive analysis of the human mycobiome. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The DAnIEL web server translates raw sequencing reads to data tables and high-standard figures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis shows differences in fungal communities between human gut, skin, nasopharynx, and oral body sites. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis shows differences in fungal communities between human skin and soil sites. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A random split of data is insufficient for benchmarking sequence analysis methods due to the non-independence of biological sequence families. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A random split of sequence data is insufficient for benchmarking sequence analysis methods due to closely related or identical test and training sequences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new algorithms successfully split more families than a previous approach, allowing for the construction of more diverse benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new algorithms successfully split more families than a previous approach, allowing for the construction of more diverse benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method for reconstructing naturalistic images and videos from brain activity demonstrates a significant improvement over existing video reconstruction techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method leverages spatial information obtained from retinotopic mappings across the visual system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method leverages spatial information obtained from retinotopic mappings across the visual system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach utilizes a fully convolutional image-to-image network trained with VGG feature loss and an adversarial regularizer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach utilizes a fully convolutional image-to-image network trained with VGG feature loss and an adversarial regularizer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Skopi is a simulation package that can generate single-hit diffraction images for reconstruction algorithms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Skopi is a simulation package that generates various types of diffraction images to support different reconstruction algorithms and machine learning classifiers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Skopi is a simulation package that generates various types of diffraction images to support different reconstruction algorithms and machine learning classifiers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Skopi is envisioned as a resource to aid the development of on-the-fly feedback during non-crystalline experiments at XFEL facilities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Skopi aids feasibility studies and advances the development of algorithms for noncrystalline experiments at XFEL facilities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: X-ray free-electron lasers (XFELs) can produce ultra-bright femtosecond X-ray pulses for coherent diffraction imaging of biomolecules. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: XFEL experiments involving aerosolized or solvated biomolecular samples present new challenges in experimental design and data processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a contextual claim about experimental challenges at XFELs that does not appear in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study defined a canonical RBC proteome and interactome using quantitative mass spectrometry and machine learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study defines a canonical RBC proteome and interactome using quantitative mass spectrometry and machine learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The RBC interactome is dominated by protein homeostasis, redox biology, cytoskeletal dynamics, and carbon metabolism. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model suggests spring-link compression of ankyrin may contribute to the characteristic RBC cell shape and flexibility. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model suggests spring-like compression of ankyrin may contribute to the characteristic RBC cell shape and flexibility. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides an in-depth view of the global protein organization of human RBCs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CurveCurator provides reliable dose-response characteristics by computing p-values and false discovery rates based on a recalibrated F-statistic and a target-decoy procedure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of CurveCurator to large-scale data sets demonstrates its scalable utility across several application areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CurveCurator demonstrates scalable utility across several application areas. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RamanSPy is an open-source Python package designed for Raman spectroscopic research and analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RamanSPy is an open-source Python package designed for Raman spectroscopic research and analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RamanSPy provides a comprehensive library of ready-to-use tools for spectroscopic analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RamanSPy provides a comprehensive library of tools that supports integrative analyses and the development of methods and protocols. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RamanSPy addresses issues of software, methodological and data standardisation in Raman spectroscopic analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RamanSPy is modular and can be readily interfaced with data science, statistical analysis, and machine learning ecosystems in Python. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RamanSPy is modular and can be interfaced with data science, statistical analysis, and machine learning ecosystems in Python. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parameters that determine domain insertion tolerance are poorly understood. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parameters determining domain insertion tolerance and allostery are poorly understood. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sequence conservation statistics are the strongest indicators of suitable insertion sites for domain insertion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sequence conservation statistics are identified as the strongest indicators of suitable insertion sites for domain insertion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over into the publication with equivalent phrasing and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study resulted in opto-chemogenetic derivatives of the transcription factor AraC that function as single-protein Boolean logic gates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental pipeline was extended to identify opto-chemogenetic derivatives of the transcription factor AraC that function as single-protein Boolean logic gates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model-based enrichment (MBE) overcomes the limitations of current approaches in sharing information across related but non-identical reads. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current approaches to characterizing differences in sequences have extremely limited ability to share information across related but non-identical reads. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MBE is based on sound theoretical principles and is easy to implement. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach, MBE, improves accuracy compared to current methods for performing differential analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-based enrichment (MBE) improves accuracy compared to current differential analysis methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current approaches to characterizing differences in biological sequences have extremely limited ability to utilize sequencing data effectively. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current methods cannot effectively use sequencing data and cannot be directly applied in many settings of interest. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can identify de novo latent transcriptional processes in squamous cell carcinomas (SCCs). (certainty=high, type=result)
