alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system achieved an approximated accuracy of 97.1% in the identification of stomata regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system achieves an approximated accuracy of 97.1% in identifying stomata regions using classifiers based on deep learning features.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric accuracy claim and attributes it to deep-learning classifiers, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification system figures out as a nearly perfect classification system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high morphological variation of stomata traits intra- and inter-species makes automatic detection and classification challenging.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces limitations and frames detection as challenging, which tempers the preprint's subjective 'nearly perfect' conclusion and revises the claim's confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The system can serve as a reference for studies using images from different cereal grasses.
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the claimed applicability to other cereal grasses, a usage not asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Warp provides real-time evaluation, correction, and processing of cryo-EM data during acquisition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Warp automates all preprocessing steps of cryo-EM data acquisition and enables real-time evaluation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the real-time evaluation claim and broadens it by emphasizing automation of all preprocessing steps, clarifying the tool's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Warp's deep learning-based particle picking algorithm rivals human accuracy, automating the pre-processing pipeline.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Warp includes deep-learning-based models for accurate particle picking and image denoising.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports deep-learning models for accurate particle picking (and adds denoising); it keeps the core claim though it uses slightly more measured wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Warp improved the nominal resolution of a cryo-EM data set for influenza virus hemagglutinin from 3.9 Å to 3.2 Å.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benchmarks show improvement in the nominal resolution of a published cryo-EM data set for influenza virus hemagglutinin, which went from 3.9 Å to 3.2 Å.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same benchmarked resolution improvement with identical numeric values.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Warp is easy to install, computationally inexpensive, and has an intuitive user interface.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or support claims about installation ease, compute cost, or UI intuitiveness, so this preprint claim does not appear in the paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Warp corrects micrographs for global and local motion and estimates the local defocus in real time.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific technical real-time correction and defocus estimation details that were not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSNR is a Deep Learning algorithm that predicts transcription factor binding locations at Single Nucleotide Resolution from DNA sequence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSNR is a Deep Learning algorithm that predicts TF binding location at Single Nucleotide Resolution de novo from DNA sequence.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core contribution claim verbatim (de novo phrasing is equivalent to the preprint description).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSNR outperforms motif search based methods for several evaluation metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSNR outperforms motif search-based methods for several evaluation metrics.
alignments.rationale: The performance superiority claim is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSNR improves the transcription factor binding site prediction specificity using ChIP-seq data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSNR improves the TFBS prediction specificity using ChIP-seq data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about improved specificity using ChIP-seq data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deconvNet architecture is constructed on top of 'DeepBind' and trained using TF-specific data from ChIP-exonuclease experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds architectural and training-data details (DeepBind base, ChIP-exo training) that were not in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cortico-basal ganglia thalamic (CBGT) network features a complex synaptic architecture that complicates the elucidation of specific population roles in evidence accumulation during decision-making.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not retain the preprint's emphasis on complexity hindering elucidation; no comparable statement appears in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variations in CBGT network properties, including subpopulation firing rates and synaptic weights, map to variability of parameters in a normative drift diffusion model (DDM).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Variations in CBGT network properties, such as subpopulation firing rates and synaptic weights, map to variability of parameters in a normative drift diffusion model (DDM).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this mapping between CBGT property variations and DDM parameter variability verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three low-dimensional control ensembles within the CBGT network impact specific qualities of the emergent decision policy: responsiveness, pliancy, and choice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three low-dimensional control ensembles within the CBGT network impact specific qualities of the emergent decision policy: responsiveness, pliancy, and choice.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result about the three control ensembles and their impacts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work establishes a mapping from neural activity in the CBGT circuit to behavioral outcomes, illuminating the importance of core sets of CBGT subnetworks in the action selection process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analyses provide mechanistic predictions about the roles of specific CBGT network elements in tuning the way that information is accumulated and translated into decision-related behavior.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's assertion as mechanistic predictions about roles in tuning accumulation and behavior, which clarifies and slightly tones the original mapping claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The cortico-basal ganglia thalamic (CBGT) network contributes to the decision-making process in mammals.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a general high-level conclusion about CBGT involvement in decision-making that is not explicitly stated as such in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comparative analysis shows that near identical structures from the same folding environment exhibit heterogeneous folding landscapes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The folding of 16 single-domain proteins from Saccharomyces cerevisiae small GTPases exhibits heterogeneous folding landscapes despite having near identical structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result but adds specific details (16 proteins, S. cerevisiae), so the core claim is clarified with more specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Yeast small GTPases unfold along different pathways either via the N- or C-terminus initiated by structure-encoded predetermined breaking points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Yeast small GTPases unfold along different pathways initiated by structure-encoded predetermined breaking points. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core finding about alternative unfolding pathways and structure-encoded break points but omits the explicit N- vs C-terminal phrasing, a minor clarification/simplification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Degrons were systematically depleted from the initial unfolding sites to protect from too rapid degradation upon spontaneous unfolding. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Degrons as recognition signals for ubiquitin-dependent degradation are systematically absent from the initial unfolding sites of the proteins studied. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the observation (absence of degrons) without asserting the protective mechanistic interpretation present in the preprint, thus revising the explanatory claim and reducing confidence in the mechanistic conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hsp70 Ssb chaperone binding correlates only with N-terminal hydrophobicity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No matching statement about Hsp70 Ssb binding specificity appears in the publication claims, so this preprint result was removed or not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deeper understanding of the interdependence of proteins with their folding environment will help to rationalize and combat disease linked to protein misfolding and dysregulation. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deeper understanding of the interdependence of proteins with their folding environment will help to rationalize and combat diseases linked to protein misfolding and dysregulation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same concluding message but expresses it with higher certainty (low -> medium) and slight wording change (plural 'diseases'), amounting to an amplified/clarified conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAsamba is a tool that predicts the coding potential of RNA molecules from sequence information using a deep-learning model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsamba is a tool that predicts the coding potential of RNA molecules from sequence information using a neural network-based model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the same contribution using the term 'neural network' rather than 'deep-learning', a terminological clarification without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAsamba mostly outperforms other state-of-the-art methods in the classification of coding and non-coding transcripts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsamba recurrently outperforms other state-of-the-art methods in classifying transcripts from humans and several other model organisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the outperforming claim but specifies recurrent performance across human and other model organisms, broadening the empirical scope compared with the preprint's general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAsamba can identify coding signals in partial-length ORFs and UTR sequences, indicating that its model is not dependent on the presence of complete coding regions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsamba can identify coding signals in partial-length ORFs and UTR sequences, indicating that its algorithm is not dependent on complete transcript sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability with slightly stronger certainty (medium -> high) and uses 'algorithm' vs 'model', so it's a clarified claim with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNAsamba is a fast and easy tool that can provide valuable contributions to genome annotation pipelines. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNAsamba will enable faster and more accurate biological findings from genomic data of species that are being sequenced for the first time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the general claim about pipeline utility to a more specific promise for newly sequenced species and adopts a lower certainty level, narrowing scope and tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the viability of integrating confidence estimation and explainability approaches for schizophrenia diagnosis using resting state functional magnetic resonance imaging data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the viability of integrating confidence estimation and explainability approaches for schizophrenia diagnosis using resting state functional magnetic resonance imaging data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MCD often adversely affects model gradients, making it ill-suited for integration with gradient-based explainability methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MCD often adversely affects model gradients, making it ill-suited for integration with gradient-based explainability methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MCBN does not affect model gradients and is more suitable for integration with gradient-based explainability methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MCBN does not affect model gradients and is more suitable for integration with gradient-based explainability methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are many participant-level differences between regular explanations and the distributions of explanations for combined explainability and confidence estimation approaches. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are many participant-level differences between regular explanations and the distributions of explanations for combined explainability and confidence estimation approaches. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A similar confidence estimation approach used in a clinical context with explanations only output for the regular model would likely not yield adequate explanations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A similar confidence estimation approach used in a clinical context with explanations only output for the regular model would likely not yield adequate explanations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of AlphaFold predictions elevates the structural coverage of human proteins from 48% to 76%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural coverage of human proteins increases from 48% to 76% when including AlphaFold predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative result with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fraction of the dark proteome is reduced from 26% to just 10% when AlphaFold models are considered. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fraction of dark proteome is reduced from 26% to just 10% when AlphaFold models are considered. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold models provide an additional coverage of 3% to 13% for disease-associated genes and mutations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold models provide an additional coverage of 3% to 13% for disease-associated genes and mutations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The contribution of AlphaFold models to the structural coverage of non-human organisms is significantly larger than that of the human proteome. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contribution of AlphaFold models to the structural coverage of non-human organisms is significantly larger than that of the human proteome. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim identical in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sequence-structure gap of human proteins has almost disappeared, which has direct consequences for knowledge on the human genome and medical applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sequence-structure gap of human proteins has almost disappeared, which has direct consequences for knowledge on the human genome and medical applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DAPTEV is able to produce structurally complex aptamers with strong binding affinities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intelligent approach DAPTEV can produce structurally complex aptamers with strong binding affinities. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about DAPTEV's capability with equivalent wording, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed AI framework can be used for the in silico design of novel aptamer drugs that can prevent the SARS-CoV-2 virus from entering human cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim asserts the specific application to in silico design of anti-SARS-CoV-2 aptamer drugs, so this contribution was removed or not included.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research explores a novel approach for the treatment of SARS-CoV-2 infection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not claim SARS-CoV-2 treatment relevance; the preprint's SARS-CoV-2-focused conclusion is absent and thus removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Impaired axon regeneration in the aged brain is not due to slowed degeneration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Regeneration of axons in aged mice is markedly reduced compared to young adults.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's combined claim about impaired regeneration is represented in the publication as a quantified reduction; the publication splits the original combined assertion into a distinct result, clarifying the magnitude of regeneration impairment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synaptic reorganization occurs following axon injury in the aged brain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased synaptic turnover in the aged brain may facilitate partial recovery from injury through synaptic re-wiring.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the observed synaptic reorganization in functional terms (possible partial recovery) rather than only reporting occurrence, and it uses more cautious language ('may'), lowering expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synaptic reorganization may serve as a compensatory mechanism after injury.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased synaptic turnover in the aged brain may facilitate partial recovery from injury through synaptic re-wiring.
alignments.rationale: Both statements posit that increased synaptic turnover/reorganization may act as a compensatory mechanism for recovery; wording and certainty are essentially preserved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Axon degeneration rates in aged mice are similar to those in young adults.
alignments.rationale: This explicit statement about degeneration rates is present in the publication but was not separately enumerated as a preprint claim (the preprint bundled causality into a single claim).

alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct changes in memory dynamics were observed in a recurrent neural network model when simulating aged versus young adult brains.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a modeling result about memory dynamics that is not represented in the preprint claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated pipeline, CShaper, quantifies morphological parameters of densely packed cells in developing C. elegans embryos.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline quantifies morphological parameters of densely packed cells in 17 developing C. elegans embryos.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same quantification capability but specifies the dataset size (17 embryos), narrowing the original, more general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CShaper generates a time-lapse framework of cellular shape and migration for C. elegans embryos from the 4-to 350-cell stage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A time-lapse 3D atlas of cell morphology for the C. elegans embryo has been generated, covering the 4- to 350-cell stages.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same claim about a time-lapse atlas covering the 4–350 cell stages; the wording is consistent and scope matches.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a quantitative resource for C. elegans early development, facilitating research in signaling transduction and cell biology of division.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The morphological atlas is expected to stimulate and enhance further studies in developmental biology, cell biology, and biomechanics.
alignments.rationale: Both claims position the resource as enabling further research; the publication lists a broader set of fields (developmental biology, biomechanics) compared with the preprint's examples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of high spatiotemporal resolution imaging techniques has inhibited the systematic characterization of cell morphology in metazoans.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this limitation as a claim; instead it focuses on the pipeline and atlas, so the preprint's stated limitation is absent from the final claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CShaper is an automatic pipeline that combines automated segmentation of fluorescently labeled membranes with automated cell lineage tracing.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a technical description of the pipeline's components (segmentation + lineage tracing) that was not explicit among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bulk2Space is a deep learning framework-based spatial deconvolution algorithm that can disclose the spatial and cellular heterogeneity of bulk RNA-seq data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bulk2Space is a deep learning framework-based spatial deconvolution algorithm that can disclose the spatial and cellular heterogeneity of bulk RNA-seq data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of bulk transcriptomics to validate Bulk2Space reveals the spatial variance of immune cells in different tumor regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of bulk transcriptomics to validate Bulk2Space reveals the spatial variance of immune cells in different tumor regions.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bulk2Space demonstrates the molecular and spatial heterogeneity of tissues during inflammation-induced tumorigenesis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bulk2Space demonstrates the molecular and spatial heterogeneity of tissues during inflammation-induced tumorigenesis.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bulk2Space has reconstructed the hierarchical structure of the mouse isocortex and annotated previously unidentified cell types in the mouse hypothalamus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bulk2Space has reconstructed the hierarchical structure of the mouse isocortex and annotated previously unidentified cell types in the mouse hypothalamus.
alignments.rationale: This specific result is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TIAToolbox is a Python toolbox designed to make Computational Pathology (CPath) more accessible to both new and advanced scientists and pathologists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TIAToolbox is a Python toolbox designed to make computational pathology accessible to computational, biomedical, and clinical researchers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the accessibility goal but broadens the target audience to include computational, biomedical, and clinical researchers rather than specifically 'new and advanced scientists and pathologists'.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TIAToolbox provides a usable and adaptable library with efficient, cutting-edge, and unit-tested tools for various stages of pathology image analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TIAToolbox provides efficient, cutting-edge, and unit-tested tools for data loading, pre-processing, model inference, post-processing, and visualization.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the stages/tools encompassed by the library, clarifying the preprint's more general description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TIAToolbox enables users to easily build upon recent deep learning developments in the Computational Pathology literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art deep-learning algorithms can be reimplemented in a streamlined manner using TIAToolbox with minimal effort.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim by emphasizing 'minimal effort' and higher certainty about streamlined reimplementation compared with the preprint's more tentative wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The user-friendly modular API of TIAToolbox allows for seamless integration of advanced deep learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interface allows for the construction of a full computational pathology deep-learning pipeline.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes building entire pipelines rather than only integrating algorithms, broadening the functional scope described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Examples demonstrate how state-of-the-art deep learning algorithms can be streamlined using TIAToolbox.
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art deep-learning algorithms can be reimplemented in a streamlined manner using TIAToolbox with minimal effort.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe streamlining SOTA algorithms; the publication expresses this with stronger certainty ('minimal effort'), increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of splicing is co-transcriptional and chromatin state affects alternative splicing.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims focus on transcription-factor regulation of intron retention and do not restate the preprint's broader claims about co-transcriptional splicing or chromatin-state effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transcription factors can regulate splicing outcomes, particularly in intron retention events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transcription factors are involved in the regulation of intron retention.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly affirms the role of transcription factors in regulating intron retention, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model predicts that the majority of transcription factors affecting intron retention belong to the zinc finger family.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of transcription factors that affect intron retention come from the zinc finger family.
alignments.rationale: The model prediction about zinc finger TF predominance is carried over as the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChIP-seq data for multiple zinc finger transcription factors shows strong over-representation of their peaks in intron retention events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is strong over-representation of zinc finger transcription factor peaks in intron retention events.
alignments.rationale: The ChIP-seq over-representation observation is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work opens up opportunities for further studies on the mechanisms by which transcription factors affect intron retention.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a forward-looking conclusion about opportunity for further mechanistic studies that is not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transcriptional profiling can systematically distinguish variants of unknown significance (VUS) as impactful vs. neutral. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transcriptional profiling can systematically distinguish variants of unknown significance as impactful vs. neutral. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with very similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cell morphological profiling (cmVIP) can predict the functional impact of variants at the single-cell level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cell morphological profiling (cmVIP) can predict the functional impact of lung adenocarcinoma-associated somatic variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the predictive claim but narrows it to lung adenocarcinoma-associated somatic variants, clarifying the context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: cmVIP profiling provides biological insights into variants that can be explored in a public online portal. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: cmVIP profiling reveals biological insights into variants at the single-cell level. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the biological-insight aspect at single-cell resolution but omits the explicit mention of a public online portal, changing the contribution's presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: cmVIP profiling is promising as a low-cost and convenient method for assessing the impact of variants on gene function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: cmVIP profiling is promising as a low-cost and convenient method to assess the impact of variants on gene function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is reproduced verbatim in the publication with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MusMorph is a database of standardized mouse morphology data that spans numerous genotypes and developmental stages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MusMorph is a database of standardized mouse morphology data that spans numerous genotypes and developmental stages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The database includes data from various developmental stages, including E10.5, E11.5, E14.5, E15.5, E18.5, and adulthood. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MusMorph is a database of standardized mouse morphology data that spans numerous genotypes and developmental stages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains that multiple developmental stages are included but omits the specific stage list, reducing specificity relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An atlas-based phenotyping pipeline was implemented to standardize data collection using techniques from image registration, deep learning, and morphometrics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The database includes phenotypic data collected using an atlas-based phenotyping pipeline that integrates image registration, deep learning, and morphometrics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the implementation of the atlas-based pipeline with the same components and purpose.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MusMorph workflow is open-source to promote transparency and reproducible data collection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The workflow for MusMorph is open-source, promoting transparency and reproducible data collection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The open-source and reproducibility claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MusMorph provides aligned micro-computed tomography images, dense anatomical landmarks, and segmentations for each specimen. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a specific data-content claim (aligned images, landmarks, segmentations) that was not explicitly stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher confidence models are enriched in interactions supported by affinity or structure-based methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We show that experiments can orthogonally confirm higher-confidence models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes experimental orthogonal confirmation of high-confidence models rather than the enrichment-by-methods framing from the preprint, representing a shift in emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify 3,137 high confidence models, of which 1,371 have no homology to a known structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify 3,137 high-confidence models, of which 1,371 have no homology to a known structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric result is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interface residues harbouring disease mutations suggest potential mechanisms for pathogenic variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify interface residues harboring disease mutations, suggesting potential mechanisms for pathogenic variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about interface residues and disease mutations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Groups of interface phosphorylation sites show patterns of co-regulation across conditions, suggestive of coordinated tuning of multiple interactions as signalling responses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Groups of interface phosphorylation sites show patterns of co-regulation across conditions, suggestive of coordinated tuning of multiple protein interactions as signaling responses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about co-regulated interface phosphorylation sites is preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate prediction of protein complexes promises to greatly expand our understanding of the atomic details of human cell biology in health and disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide examples of how the predicted binary complexes can be used to build larger assemblies helping to expand our understanding of human cell biology. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives concrete examples of using predicted complexes to build assemblies (clarifying application) but expresses this as contribution-level examples with somewhat lower certainty than the preprint's broad promise.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phase-contrast x-ray tomography has been used to characterize the three-dimensional structure of cardiac tissue from patients who succumbed to Covid-19. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phase-contrast X-ray tomography can characterize the three-dimensional structure of cardiac tissue from patients who succumbed to Covid-19. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim with the same focus and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pathological alterations of vessels in Covid-19 patients indicate an elevated occurrence of intussusceptive angiogenesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pathological alterations of vessels in Covid-19 patients indicate an elevated occurrence of intussusceptive angiogenesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about elevated intussusceptive angiogenesis is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The histopathology of Covid-19 differs from both influenza and typical coxsackie virus myocarditis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The histopathology of Covid-19 differs from both influenza and typical coxsackie virus myocarditis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same comparative histopathology conclusion with equal certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid method, SAE-IBS, achieves higher accuracy than existing methods for projecting poor quality target samples onto a reference ancestry space. (certainty=high, type=result)
