alignments.pub_claim_text: The hourglass organization of the connectome suggests similarities with encoder-decoder artificial neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same analogy between the hourglass organization and encoder-decoder networks with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The set of core interneurons remains mostly the same when considering only chemical synapses or a combination of chemical synapses and gap junctions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The set of core interneurons remains mostly the same when considering only chemical synapses or the combination of chemical synapses and gap junctions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same robustness result regarding core interneuron identity across synapse types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Light intensity and spectral composition differentially affect the diel molecular responses of Kale (Brassica oleracea). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Light intensity and spectral composition significantly influence the growth and physiology of Kale (Brassica oleracea). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's molecular/diel framing to broader growth and physiology but conveys the same experimental finding about light effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify time-of-day and cultivar-specific light intensity and spectral composition-induced changes related to growth, shade avoidance, photosynthesis, and several aspects of nutritional composition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Time-of-day and cultivar-specific light conditions induce changes related to growth, shade avoidance, photosynthesis, and nutritional composition in kale. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same set of time-of-day and cultivar-specific responses with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate the translational potential of light manipulation in tailoring plant growth and nutritional content for enhanced crop productivity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the translational potential of light manipulation in enhancing kale growth and nutritional content. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim translational potential of light manipulation for improving kale growth and nutrition with similar emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PSZ with high motivational deficits made increasingly less optimal choices as the difference in reward value between two competing stimuli increased. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PSZ with high motivational deficits made increasingly less optimal choices as the difference in reward value between two competing stimuli increased. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same behavioral result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PSZ relied less on expected value (EV) and more on stimulus-response learning, which correlated with SANS motivational deficit severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PSZ relied less on expected value (EV) and more on stimulus-response learning, which correlated with motivational deficit severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint's behavioral and correlation finding with essentially identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Probability-magnitude deficits in PSZ with motivational deficits arise from underutilization of EV in favor of reliance on value-less stimulus-response associations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probability-magnitude deficits in PSZ with motivational deficits arise from underutilization of EV in favor of reliance on valueless stimulus-response associations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same explanatory conclusion about underutilization of EV and reliance on valueless stimulus-response associations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Probability-magnitude integration deficits were driven specifically by a failure to represent reward magnitude. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probability-magnitude integration deficits were driven specifically by a failure to represent reward magnitude. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same mechanistic conclusion regarding failure to represent reward magnitude.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ProCare computational approach identified a possible similarity in the binding pockets of human tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-α) and HIV-1 reverse transcriptase (HIV-1 RT).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The similarity between the binding pockets of human TNF-α and HIV-1 RT was not revealed by state-of-the-art binding sites comparison methods or ligand-based pairwise similarity searches.
alignments.rationale: Both statements concern the specific TNF-α/HIV-1 RT pocket similarity; the publication adds context about other methods failing to reveal it, thus clarifying the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Microscale thermophoresis experiments confirmed that two of the three tested and FDA-approved HIV-1 RT inhibitors bind to soluble human TNF-α trimer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Microscale thermophoresis experiments confirmed that two of the three tested FDA-approved HIV-1 RT inhibitors bind to soluble human TNF-α trimer.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The point cloud registration approach implemented in ProCare is uniquely suited to identify local and unobvious similarities among totally unrelated targets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The point cloud registration approach implemented in ProCare is uniquely suited to identify local and unobvious similarities among totally unrelated targets.
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim about ProCare's point cloud registration is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Considering local similarities can efficiently guide drug discovery, particularly in repurposing existing drug candidates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ProCare computational approach can identify local similarities in fragment-based subpockets of druggable protein cavities.
alignments.rationale: The preprint makes an application-oriented claim about repurposing; the publication focuses on method capability to find local similarities without the explicit repurposing/application claim, thus narrowing and tempering the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: M ustache detects loops at a wide range of genomic distances, identifying potential structural and regulatory interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mustache is a new method for multi-scale detection of chromatin loops from Hi-C and Micro-C contact maps.
alignments.rationale: Both describe Mustache as a multi-scale loop detection method across genomic distances; the publication frames this as the method's contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: M ustache reports two to three times the number of HiCCUPS loops, which are reproducible across replicates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mustache reports loops that are highly consistent between replicates and between Hi-C and Micro-C datasets.
alignments.rationale: The preprint gives a specific quantitative increase versus HiCCUPS; the publication emphasizes reproducibility/consistency without the explicit two- to three-fold numeric claim, so the claim is revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: M ustache recovers a larger proportion of published ChIA-PET and HiChIP loops than HiCCUPS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mustache recovers a higher number of published ChIA-PET and HiChIP loops compared to other loop callers like HiCCUPS and SIP.
alignments.rationale: Both claim Mustache recovers more published ChIA-PET/HiChIP loops, with the publication generalizing the comparator set but preserving the substantive result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Micro-C provides better power for loop detection compared to Hi-C.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make an explicit claim that Micro-C provides better power than Hi-C; that specific comparative claim is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: M ustache enables a more efficient and comprehensive analysis of the chromatin looping from high-resolution Hi-C and Micro-C datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mustache enables an efficient and comprehensive analysis of chromatin loops.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's conclusion about enabling efficient and comprehensive loop analysis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CNCB model introduces two main parameters, confidence noise and confidence boost, to interpret confidence ratings in perceptual decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNCB model introduces two main parameters, confidence noise and confidence boost, to interpret confidence rating judgments.
alignments.rationale: Both versions introduce the same two parameters for interpreting confidence judgments; wording differs only minimally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Confidence noise negatively impacts confidence judgments beyond the effects of sensory noise on perceptual sensitivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Confidence noise impairs confidence judgments beyond the effects of sensory noise on perceptual sensitivity.
alignments.rationale: The experimental/result claim about confidence noise is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The confidence boost parameter indicates whether confidence relies on the same information used for perceptual decisions or on new information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNCB model introduces two main parameters, confidence noise and confidence boost, to interpret confidence rating judgments.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the boost parameter as part of the model description but does not explicitly articulate the interpretive role attributed in the preprint, so the claim is present but less elaborated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CNCB model provides a theory-driven alternative to the M-ratio for estimating confidence efficiency measures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNCB model provides a theory-driven alternative to the popular M-ratio for estimating confidence efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim that CNCB offers an alternative to M-ratio for estimating confidence efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be extended to continuous confidence ratings and allows for the estimation of a non-linear mapping between objective and subjective confidence probabilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The continuous confidence model includes a non-linear mapping between objective and subjective confidence probabilities that can be estimated.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the continuous extension and estimable non-linear mapping; the publication states this result clearly.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A toolbox is provided to help researchers estimate all parameters of the CNCB model in confidence ratings datasets.
alignments.rationale: The toolbox is a new practical resource reported in the publication that was not mentioned in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies a kinematic mechanism driving shape change in multi-layer organ formation due to tissue layer interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The in-plane shape change linked to out-of-plane organ folding is influenced by tissue incompressibility and initial organ geometry.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe mechanical/kinematic contributors to organ shape change; the publication emphasizes specific biophysical influences (incompressibility and geometry), clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Acute perturbations of contractility in the muscle layer reveal that contraction patterns are due to muscle activity, which induces cell shape changes in the adjacent endoderm layer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Muscle contractions induce cell shape change in the adjacent tissue layer, driving a convergent extension pattern.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result that muscle contractions induce neighboring cell shape changes is preserved and restated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The induction cascade of cell shape changes relies on high frequency calcium pulses in the muscle layer, controlled by hox genes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hox genes control the emergence of high-frequency calcium pulses that trigger muscle contractions.
alignments.rationale: Both claims link Hox genes to high-frequency calcium pulses in muscle that drive contractions; the publication frames this as a control relationship consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibition of calcium targets involved in myosin phosphorylation abolishes constrictions in the tissue layers.
alignments.rationale: The specific experimental claim about inhibition of calcium targets abolishing constrictions does not appear among the publication claims provided.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reveals how genetic patterning in one layer triggers a dynamic molecular mechanism to control a physical process in the adjacent layer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings reveal a mechanical route for gene expression to induce organ shape change through post-translational mechanisms.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conceptual conclusion that gene expression in one layer drives mechanical/physical changes in another layer via molecular (post-translational) mechanisms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A particular set of unrealistically pessimistic assumptions can distort an agent’s behavior and underlie a host of seemingly disparate anxiety symptoms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A particular set of unrealistically pessimistic assumptions can distort an agent's behavior and underlie a host of seemingly disparate anxiety symptoms.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about pessimistic assumptions underlying anxiety symptoms is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A violation of the assumption that potential for avoidance should be protective leads to excessive propagation of fear and avoidance to situations far antecedent of threat.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A violation of the assumption that later avoidance will be successful leads to excessive propagation of fear and avoidance to situations far antecedent of threat.
alignments.rationale: The two statements convey the same model mechanism (a violated avoidance-success assumption leading to propagation of fear), differing only in wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations of the model reproduce laboratory demonstrations of abnormal decision making in anxiety, including in situations of approach-avoid conflict and planning to avoid losses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations of the model reproduce laboratory demonstrations of abnormal decision making in anxiety, including in situations of approach-avoid conflict and planning to avoid losses.
alignments.rationale: The simulation results are restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning under the pessimistic bias captures a hypothesis about the role of anxiety in the later development of depression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning under the pessimistic bias captures a hypothesis about the role of anxiety in the later development of depression.
alignments.rationale: The interpretive claim linking anxiety-related learning biases to later depression is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The bias offers a new formalization of classic insights from the psychiatric literature about the central role of maladaptive beliefs about control and self-efficacy in anxiety.
alignments.rationale: The specific framing of the bias as a new formalization of classic psychiatric insights is not present among the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: iSeq is the only tool that supports simultaneous retrieval from multiple databases including GSA, SRA, ENA, DDBJ, and GEO. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: iSeq supports simultaneous retrieval from GSA, SRA, ENA, and DDBJ databases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms simultaneous retrieval from several databases but omits GEO and drops the exclusivity claim (“only tool”), thus narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: iSeq supports at least 25 different accession formats as input. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention the number or variety of accession formats, so this specific format-support claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: iSeq features parallel downloads, multi-threaded processes, FASTQ file merging, and integrity verification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes retrieval capability generally but does not list these specific implementation features, so the detailed feature claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The KGT framework significantly reduces factual errors in reasoning by integrating LLMs with Knowledge Graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The KGT framework significantly reduces factual errors in reasoning by integrating LLMs with knowledge graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this primary result with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KGT can facilitate the discovery of new uses for existing drugs through potential drug-cancer associations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KGT can facilitate the discovery of new uses for existing drugs through potential drug-cancer associations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion verbatim, with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The KGT framework substantially improves the accuracy and utility of LLMs in the biomedical field. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The KGT framework substantially improves the accuracy and utility of LLMs in the biomedical field. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same high-level conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have proposed the first KGQA benchmark in the field of biomedicine utilizing a pan-cancer knowledge graph. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim states this is the first KGQA pan-cancer benchmark; the publication instead frames results and proof-of-concept without making that novelty claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Case studies reveal our method enhanced LLMs in addressing biomedical challenges such as drug repositioning and resistance research. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study serves as a proof of concept for the exceptional performance of KGT in biomedical question answering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the case-study outcomes as a broader proof-of-concept for strong performance in biomedical QA, which is a somewhat narrower framing of specific case-study applications and stated with stronger certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed query engine increases the potential of AOP exploration by reducing the time, human resources, and technical ability needed to make practical use of the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The present query engine reduces the time and technical requirements for utilizing the data within AOP-Wiki. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms reduction in time and technical requirements; it omits explicit mention of human resources but conveys the same practical reduction in barriers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dynamic prompt generation capability of the query engine will lead to precise query generation as the interaction increases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report on dynamic prompt generation improving precision over interaction, so this forward-looking claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptation of AOP wiki data into a Labeled Property Graph (LPG) database enhances the usability of the AOP network data for risk assessment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work proposes to adapt the AOP-Wiki data into a Labelled Property Graph (LPG) schema to enhance its practical use in risk assessment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution is preserved (LPG adaptation to improve use in risk assessment), though the publication frames it with slightly lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AOP-wiki platform currently lacks a versatile querying system necessary for practical use in risk assessment. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AOP-Wiki platform currently lacks a versatile querying system, which hinders developers' exploration of the AOP network. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this limitation closely, with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LMLF can guide both LLMs to generate potential leads starting with the same logical constraints and query text. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LMLF can be used to guide both LLMs to generate potential leads starting with the same logical constraints and query text. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Binding affinities of LMLF-generated molecules are skewed towards higher binding affinities than those from existing baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Binding affinities of LMLF-generated molecules are skewed towards higher binding affinities than those from existing baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this quantitative comparison directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LMLF results in generating molecules that are skewed towards higher binding affinities than without logical feedback. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LMLF results in generating molecules that are skewed towards higher binding affinities than without logical feedback. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Assessment by a computational chemist suggests that LMLF generated compounds may be novel inhibitors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Assessment by a computational chemist suggests that LMLF generated compounds may be novel inhibitors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same expert assessment with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs with logical feedback may provide a mechanism for generating new leads without requiring the domain-specialist to acquire sophisticated skills in prompt-engineering. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state this claim about lowering the need for specialist prompt-engineering skills, so the specific asserted implication is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The calcium activity of somatostatin (SST) interneurons in the primary motor cortex exhibits highly action-locked and synchronized calcium responses during the acquisition of a single lever-press task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: M1 somatostatin interneurons (SST-Ins) display synchronized, action-locked calcium activity during acquisition of a lever-press task in freely moving mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core experimental observation with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: After extended training, M1 SST IN activity was no longer related to action execution, but redistributed when the training schedule was updated. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Following extended training under a stable task schedule, SST-IN activity was no longer coupled to action execution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the loss of coupling after extended training (Q1). The redistribution aspect is addressed separately in Q2, so the main portion of this claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The redistributed calcium activity of M1 SST INs correlated with structural modulation of ongoing action sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When task demands were modified to require faster and more temporally constrained action sequences, SST-IN activity redistributed and correlated with trial-by-trial changes in sequences. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same redistribution-and-correlation result but frames it with slightly lower certainty and specifies the conditions (modified task demands).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibition of SST INs disrupted the temporal structure of action sequences and decreased the efficiency of motor program execution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inhibition of SST-INs disrupted temporal organization and impaired efficient motor execution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same causal disruption result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: M1 SST interneurons play an unexpected role in actively refining motor programs into more efficient and task-specific structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings highlight the unexpected role of M1 SST-INs in refining motor programs into more efficient and task-specific structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same concluding interpretation with matching scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The entorhinal cortex integrates relational information about changing values by representing an abstract value space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The entorhinal grid system supports the prediction of future values by representing a cognitive map.
alignments.rationale: Both claims link entorhinal representations and value, but the publication reframes the function toward prediction of future values (cognitive map supporting prediction) rather than the broader integration of relational information, and the publication has lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants successfully integrated and extrapolated changes along the two value dimensions during the decision-making task.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim reports the specific behavioral result about participants integrating and extrapolating the two value dimensions; the behavioral claim does not appear in the publication claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The entorhinal cortex exhibited a grid-like representation aligned to the most informative axis through the value space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The entorhinal cortex exhibited a grid-like representation aligned to the axis through the value space most informative for choices.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with near-identical wording and emphasis on alignment to the most informative axis for choices.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A network of brain regions, including the ventromedial prefrontal cortex, tracked the prospective value difference between options.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A network of brain regions, including the ventromedial prefrontal cortex, tracked the prospective value difference between options.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same neural-network tracking result but with higher stated certainty, indicating stronger confidence in the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings provide novel insight for our understanding of cognitive maps as a mechanism to guide prospective decision making in humans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cognitive map might be used to generate lower-dimensional value signals to guide prospective decision making.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the broader contribution more specific by suggesting cognitive maps generate lower-dimensional value signals to guide decisions, narrowing the general 'novel insight' statement into a mechanistic suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dysfunction in learning and motivational systems contributes to addictive behaviours.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dysfunction in learning and motivational systems contributes to addictive behaviours.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion about dysfunction contributing to addictive behaviors with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed mathematical definition of salience combines dopamine's roles in learning and motivation within the reinforcement learning framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed mathematical definition of salience combines aspects of dopamine's role in both learning and motivation within the reinforcement learning framework.
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the same methodological/conceptual contribution — a mathematical salience definition linking dopamine's roles in learning and motivation within RL.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current salience model simulates the downweighting of drug-related negative prediction-errors and steeper delay discounting of drug rewards.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current salience model simulates addictive behaviours and accounts for the downweighting of drug-related negative prediction-errors and steeper delay discounting of drug rewards.
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the result to claim the model simulates addictive behaviours more broadly while retaining the specific simulated phenomena; this is a substantive broadening of the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The salience mechanism may modulate internal representation updating and contribute to addictive behaviours.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model suggests that learning and motivational aspects of dopaminergic functioning interact through a salience mechanism that modulates internal representation updating.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the mechanistic interaction and modulation of internal updating but omits the explicit tentative link to addictive behaviours, narrowing the downstream claim while clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Previous models of dopaminergic roles in addiction have significant limitations in explaining key hallmarks of addictive behaviours.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly restates or retains the preprint's critical claim about limitations of previous models, so this comparative limitation claim is absent from the publication claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recall-gated consolidation significantly amplifies the signal-to-noise ratio for memory storage in noisy environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recall-gated consolidation provides significant advantages by substantially amplifying the signal-to-noise ratio with which memories can be stored in noisy environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core result about substantial SNR amplification for memory storage in noisy environments with equivalent high certainty and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model captures common computational principles underlying learning and memory formation across various species and contexts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model captures common computational principles underlying the selective consolidation of experiences into long-term memory.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the claim to selective consolidation into long-term memory (rather than the broader cross-species/context generality) and states it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recall-gated consolidation leads to phenomena observed in behavioral learning paradigms, such as spaced learning effects and task-dependent rates of consolidation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recall-gated consolidation gives rise to phenomena present in behavioral learning paradigms, including spaced learning effects and task-dependent rates of consolidation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the behavioral phenomena claim but with slightly lower stated certainty, so the content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model includes neural circuit implementations for different types of learning problems, influenced by factors like prediction accuracy and decision confidence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model includes neural circuit implementations for different types of learning problems, such as supervised learning and reinforcement learning.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe neural circuit implementations, but the publication replaces the emphasis on modulatory factors (prediction accuracy, decision confidence) with explicit examples of learning problem types, altering the specifics of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed computational framework allows biological and artificial agents to flexibly couple and decouple neural task modules for cognitive processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel computational framework allows biological and artificial agents to flexibly couple and decouple neural task modules for cognitive processing.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution claim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates significant computational advantages over the original network in both stability and plasticity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates significant computational advantages over the original network without synchrony, in terms of both stability and plasticity.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the comparison condition ('without synchrony'), clarifying the baseline against which advantages were demonstrated while preserving the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting models’ processing dynamics are consistent with empirical data and provide empirically testable hypotheses for future MEG/EEG studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The processing dynamics of the resulting model are consistent with empirical data and provide empirically testable hypotheses for future MEG/EEG studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same empirical-consistency and hypothesis-generating claim but reports it with slightly lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework combines computational efficiency with biological plausibility to address the stability-plasticity dilemma.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly restates the specific theoretical claim about jointly addressing the stability-plasticity dilemma through combined computational efficiency and biological plausibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel vision-based collective motion with collision avoidance model (VCMCA) is proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel vision-based collective motion with collision avoidance model (VCMCA) is proposed.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm was evaluated in the case of locusts’ swarms, demonstrating the evolution of collective motion and collision avoidance behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm demonstrates the evolution of collective motion and collision avoidance behaviors in locust swarms.
alignments.rationale: The result about evaluation in locust swarms and demonstration of collective motion and collision avoidance is preserved with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learning process of individual agents leads to the emergence of global collective motion dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learning agent in the VCMCA model learns to perform local collective motion with collision avoidance through trial-and-error.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes learning of local collective motion via trial-and-error rather than asserting emergence of global collective dynamics, narrowing and altering the original emergence claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nature-inspired genetic algorithms and reinforcement-learning methods enable agents to implement collective behavior similar to that found in nature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work presents a biologically-inspired learning process for achieving multi-agent multi-objective dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the algorithmic approach as a biologically-inspired learning process for multi-agent dynamics, which aligns with and clarifies the preprint's claim about nature-inspired GAs and RL enabling naturalistic collective behavior.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The successor representation is a promising formalization of the predictive map hypothesis for hippocampal function. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The successor representation is a formalization of the predictive map hypothesis that proposes each place cell encodes the expected state occupancy of its target location in the near future. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core claim but elaborates the proposed coding interpretation (place cell expected occupancy), so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spike-timing dependent plasticity (STDP) acting on theta sweeps is sufficient to rapidly learn a close approximation to the successor representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spike-timing dependent plasticity (STDP) acting on temporally compressed trajectories is sufficient to rapidly learn a close approximation to the successor representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses the term 'temporally compressed trajectories' in place of 'theta sweeps' (an explicative rephrasing) while preserving the original result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model is biologically plausible and uses spiking neurons modulated by theta-band oscillations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model developed in this study is biologically plausible and uses spiking neurons modulated by theta-band oscillations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim but with stronger stated confidence, so content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model explains substantial variance in the temporal difference successor matrix and accounts for experimentally observed phenomena related to the successor representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model explains substantial variance in the temporal difference successor matrix and accounts for experimentally observed phenomena related to the successor representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result verbatim; there is no substantive change in content, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model provides insight into the topographical ordering of place field sizes along the dorsal-ventral axis, which is necessary to prevent detrimental mixing of place fields. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provides insight into the topographical ordering of place field sizes along the dorsal-ventral axis, which is necessary to prevent detrimental mixing of larger place fields with more fine-grained predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the original wording to specify the nature of the detrimental mixing (larger fields with fine-grained predictions), so the claim is clarified without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Losartan improved choice accuracy for the hardest stimulus pair by increasing expected value sensitivity towards the rewarding stimulus relative to the placebo group during learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Losartan improved choice accuracy for the hardest stimulus pair by increasing expected value sensitivity towards the rewarding stimulus relative to the placebo group during learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result directly with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Losartan reduced the learning rate for negative outcomes and increased exploitatory choice behaviors while preserving learning for positive outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational modeling revealed that losartan reduced the learning rate for negative outcomes and increased exploitatory choice behaviors while preserving learning for positive outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit method qualifier ('computational modeling revealed') while preserving the substantive result, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Losartan increased RPE signaling in orbitofrontal-striatal regions and enhanced positive outcome representations in the ventral striatum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a claim about increased RPE signaling or enhanced positive-outcome representations in OFC-striatal areas; that specific mechanistic claim is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Losartan accelerated response times and enhanced VS functional connectivity with left dorsolateral prefrontal cortex when approaching maximum rewards. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Losartan accelerated response times and enhanced VS functional connectivity with left dorsolateral prefrontal cortex when approaching maximum rewards in the transfer phase. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the context that the effect was observed in the transfer phase and reports a lower certainty level; the core result remains but is clarified and presented more cautiously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings indicate a promising therapeutic mechanism to normalize distorted reward learning and fronto-striatal functioning in depression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This may indicate a promising therapeutic mechanism to normalize distorted reward learning and fronto-striatal functioning in depression. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the conclusion ('may indicate') and lowers confidence, so the claim is retained but clarified and presented with reduced confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chronic distress induced by oral corticosterone alters behavioural initiation, effort allocation, and hedonic appreciation in mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chronic distress induced by oral corticosterone treatment alters behavioural initiation, effort allocation, and hedonic appreciation in mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental result with equivalent content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Corticosterone treatment leads to a dampening of neural activation in the anterior insular cortex and basolateral amygdala. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Corticosterone treatment leads to dampened neural activation in the anterior insular cortex and basolateral amygdala, which are key regions for motivational processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds interpretive context (these regions are key for motivational processing) but preserves the core neural finding, so it is a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chronic distress results in maladaptive decision-making under uncertainty due to irregular neural activations in key brain regions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chronic distress-induced irregular neural activations in the aIC and BLA could result in maladaptive decision-making under uncertainty. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the aIC and BLA as the implicated regions (narrowing) and frames the link as a potential ('could result') rather than definitive causal language, so the claim is clarified and somewhat tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study illustrates how the balance of effort and reward processing adjusts the motivational threshold for goal-directed behaviours. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study illustrates how effort and reward processing adjust the motivational threshold for goal-directed behaviours in varying environments. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the context 'in varying environments,' which broadens the situational scope while preserving the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher VS-hippocampal resting-state functional connectivity at baseline predicted greater substance use at follow up. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher VS-hippocampal resting-state functional connectivity at baseline predicted greater substance use exposure at follow up. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same predictive association, only adding the term 'exposure' but not altering the claim's substance or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides the first evidence linking increased VS-hippocampal connectivity with greater adolescent substance use. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These data provide the first evidence linking increased VS-hippocampal connectivity with greater adolescent substance use exposure. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same novelty claim (first evidence) with equivalent confidence; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adolescent substance use is driven by a heightened sensitivity to reward and a stronger link between reinforcement learning and episodic memory for rewarding outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Variation in adolescent substance use may relate to individual differences in mesolimbic sensitivity to reward and memory sensitivity to reward. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from a stronger causal 'is driven by' formulation to a correlational, tentative 'may relate to individual differences,' which revises the claim to be more cautious.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurexin1α knockout animals exhibited blunted choice bias towards outcomes associated with greater benefits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurexin1α knockouts exhibited reduced selection of beneficial outcomes and impaired avoidance of costlier options. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a highly similar behavioral deficit (reduced selection of beneficial outcomes); wording differs but the substantive result is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mutant mice were impaired in avoiding costlier, benefit-neutral actions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurexin1α knockouts exhibited reduced selection of beneficial outcomes and impaired avoidance of costlier options. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly mentions impaired avoidance of costlier options, directly matching this preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deficits in the updating and representation of choice values largely drove the observed behavioral patterns in the mutant mice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deficits in value-based decision-making are related to region-specific Nrxn1α disruption and changes in value-modulated neural activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication ties behavioral deficits to region-specific gene disruption and altered value-modulated activity, which clarifies and narrows the mechanistic attribution compared with the broader preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurexin1α disruption within forebrain excitatory projection neurons recapitulated most aspects of the whole-brain knockout phenotype. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Disruption of Neurexin1α within telencephalic excitatory projection neurons recapitulated choice abnormalities of global Neurexin1α knockouts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same finding (selective excitatory projection neuron disruption recapitulates global phenotype) using equivalent terminology (telencephalic ~ forebrain).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selective knockout of Neurexin1α within forebrain excitatory neurons disrupts reward-associated neural signals within the striatum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The selective forebrain excitatory knockout of Neurexin1α perturbed value-modulated neural signals within the striatum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same neural-signal disruption finding in the striatum (value-modulated signals), so the claim is unchanged in substance and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STaCker is a deep learning algorithm that unifies the coordinates of transcriptomic slices via an image registration process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STaCker is a deep learning algorithm that unifies the coordinates of transcriptomic slices via an image registration process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same description and contribution as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STaCker demonstrates a significant increase in spatial concordance in aligned slices compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STaCker demonstrates a significant increase in spatial concordance in aligned slices compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STaCker successfully harmonizes multiple real spatial transcriptome datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STaCker successfully harmonizes multiple real spatial transcriptome datasets acquired from various platforms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STaCker is applicable to any tissue type and overcomes the training data scarcity by being trained exclusively on diverse synthetic data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not repeat the claims about universal tissue applicability or exclusive training on synthetic data; instead it presents a different concluding use-case.

alignments.pub_claim_text: STaCker is a valuable computational tool for constructing a common coordinate framework with spatial transcriptome data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about CCF construction appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Fast-Optimized Network for Denoising through residual Unified Ensembles (FONDUE) method demonstrated stable denoising capabilities across multiple resolutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Fast-Optimized Network for Denoising through residual Unified Ensembles (FONDUE) method demonstrated stable denoising capabilities across multiple resolutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FONDUE's performance is comparable to state-of-the-art methods for denoising structural human brain MRIs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FONDUE's performance is on par or superior to state-of-the-art methods while being several orders of magnitude faster at low relative cost when using a dedicated Graphics Processing Unit (GPU). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim (allowing superiority) and adds speed/cost advantages, revising and broadening the original comparative statement and increasing confidence in performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FONDUE was capable of denoising 0.5mm 3 isotropic T1w images in under 3 minutes on an NVIDIA RTX 3090 GPU using less than 8GB of video memory. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Due to its high-quality performance, robustness, fast execution times, and relatively low-GPU memory requirements, FONDUE can be widely used for structural MRI denoising, especially in large-cohort studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific runtime/memory benchmark from the preprint is generalized in the publication into broader claims about speed and low memory needs; the concrete numeric claim is not reproduced and the publication frames it more as a conclusion with tempered certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher-resolution MRI images contain greater amounts of noise contamination, leading to poorer quality brain morphometry if not addressed adequately. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate this broader conclusion about high-resolution noise leading to poorer morphometry, so it appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: FONDUE achieved the best performance on at least one of the four denoising-performance metrics on all the test datasets used. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific quantitative-best-performance claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The switch to neurogenesis in neural stem and progenitor cells (NPCs) is gradual and shows considerable heterogeneity in individual NPC neurogenic potential and division rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The switch to neurogenesis within the first 24 hours of zebrafish telencephalon development is gradual. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A quantitative atlas detailing the production of telencephalic neurons and NPC division modes between 14 and 24 hours post-fertilization has been established. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide a quantitative atlas detailing the production of telencephalic neurons and NPC division modes between 14 and 24 hours postfertilization. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a basis for future research aimed at understanding the molecular mechanisms and regulators of early neurogenesis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study establishes a basis for future research aimed at understanding the molecular mechanisms and regulators of early neurogenesis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same forward-looking claim but frames it with higher certainty and as a contribution, thus amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PLMGraph-Inter outperforms five top inter-protein contact prediction methods by large margins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PLMGraph-Inter outperforms five top inter-protein contact prediction methods by large margins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction of PLMGraph-Inter can complement the result of AlphaFold-Multimer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction of PLMGraph-Inter can complement the result of AlphaFold-Multimer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this concluding observation unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Leveraging the contacts predicted by PLMGraph-Inter as constraints for protein-protein docking can dramatically improve its performance for protein complex structure prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Leveraging the contacts predicted by PLMGraph-Inter as constraints for protein-protein docking can dramatically improve its performance for protein complex structure prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this specific result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-mRNA splicing represses a proportion (4% to 32%) of m 6 A deposition at nearby exons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exon-intron boundary represses a proportion (12% to 34%) of m6A deposition at adjacent exons. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental validation confirms that the m 6 A signal increases in mRNA once the host gene does not undergo pre-mRNA splicing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: m6A signal increases once the host gene does not undergo pre-mRNA splicing to produce the same mRNA. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-mRNA splicing inhibited m 6 A sites tend to have higher m 6 A enhancers and lower m 6 A silencers locally than the m 6 A sites that are not inhibited. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inhibited m6A sites have higher m6A enhancers and lower m6A silencers locally, showing high heterogeneity at different exons genome-widely. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The m 6 A deposition inhibition by pre-mRNA splicing shows high heterogeneity at different exons of mRNAs genome-wide. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inhibited m6A sites have higher m6A enhancers and lower m6A silencers locally, showing high heterogeneity at different exons genome-widely. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: m6A is majorly responsible for why mRNAs with more exons are more stable. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Exon junction complex (EJC) only partially contributes to exon-intron boundary m6A inhibition in some short internal exons. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SingleMod is a deep learning model designed for precise m6A modification mapping on individual RNA molecules from direct RNA sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SingleMod achieves ROC AUC and PR AUC of ~0.95 for single-molecule m6A prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of SingleMod to human cell lines reveals the transcriptome-wide m6A landscape at single-molecule and single-base resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SingleMod allows for the systematic dissection of the transcriptome-wide m6A landscape at single-molecule and single-base resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple m6A sites on an RNA molecule can cumulatively influence its splicing and stability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comparative analyses across eight diverse species quantitatively elucidate three distinct m6A distribution patterns that suggest divergent regulatory mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study quantitatively elucidates three distinct m6A distribution patterns correlated with phylogenetic relationships. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides a novel framework for understanding the shaping of the epitranscriptome from a single-molecule perspective. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SingleMod allows for the systematic dissection of the transcriptome-wide m6A landscape at single-molecule and single-base resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NAPS is a hybrid tracking framework that combines deep learning-based methods for pose estimation with unique markers for identity persistence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NAPS is a hybrid tracking framework that combines deep learning-based methods for pose estimation with unique markers for identity persistence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NAPS allows the exploration of the social dynamics of the common eastern bumblebee (Bombus impatiens). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NAPS allows the exploration of the social dynamics of the common eastern bumblebee (Bombus impatiens). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NAPS can scale to long timescale experiments at a high frame rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NAPS can scale to long timescale experiments at a high frame rate and enables the investigation of detailed behavioural variation within individuals in a group. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NAPS enables the investigation of detailed behavioral variation within individuals in a group. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NAPS can scale to long timescale experiments at a high frame rate and enables the investigation of detailed behavioural variation within individuals in a group. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Expanding the toolkit for capturing the constituent behaviors of social groups is essential for understanding the structure and dynamics of social networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Expanding the toolkit for capturing the constituent behaviours of social groups is essential for understanding the structure and dynamics of social networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this broader interpretive conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrative analysis identified 107 distinct cell types and revealed the cell-type-specific usage of 544,735 candidate cis-regulatory DNA elements (cCREs) in the human genome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified 107 distinct cell types and their specific utilization of 544,735 candidate cis-regulatory DNA elements in the human genome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this main integrative result with essentially the same numbers and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nearly 40% of cCREs were human specific, with chromatin accessibility associated with species-restricted gene expression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nearly a third of the candidate cis-regulatory DNA elements demonstrated conservation and chromatin accessibility in mouse brain cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim changes the emphasis and magnitude (from ~40% human-specific to ~33% conserved in mouse brain), revising the cross-species interpretation and narrowing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We uncovered strong associations between specific brain cell types and neuropsychiatric disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are strong links between specific brain cell types and neuropsychiatric disorders including schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, Alzheimer's disease, and major depression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed deep learning models to predict regulatory function of non-coding disease risk variants. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models were developed to predict the regulatory roles of noncoding risk variants in neuropsychiatric disorders. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the application to neuropsychiatric disorders specifically, clarifying the models' target use while preserving the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBPNet predicts CLIP crosslink count distribution from RNA sequence at single-nucleotide resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBPNet predicts CLIP-seq crosslink count distribution from RNA sequence at single-nucleotide resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same prediction capability at single-nucleotide resolution with the same focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBPNet achieves high generalization on eCLIP, iCLIP and miCLIP assays, outperforming state-of-the-art classifiers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBPNet achieves high generalization on eCLIP, iCLIP and miCLIP assays, outperforming state-of-the-art classifiers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance/generalization claim is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBPNet performs bias correction by modeling the raw signal as a mixture of the protein-specific and background signal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBPNet improves imputation of protein-RNA interactions and mechanistic interpretation of predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBPNet identifies predictive sub-sequences corresponding to known binding motifs and enables variant-impact scoring via in silico mutagenesis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBPNet identifies predictive sub-sequences that correspond to known and novel binding motifs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
