alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 can predict protein structures with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFold2 can predict protein structures with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same high-level conclusion about AlphaFold2's accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ΔΔG values for ligand transformations calculated with FEP using AlphaFold2 structures are comparable in accuracy to those calculated using X-ray structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ΔΔG values for ligand transformations calculated with FEP using AlphaFold2 structures are comparable in accuracy to those calculated using crystal structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same comparative FEP result using equivalent terminology (crystal vs X-ray structures).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFold2 modeled structures are accurate enough to be used by physics-based methods such as FEP in typical lead optimization stages of a drug discovery program. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Under the right circumstances, AlphaFold2-modeled structures are accurate enough to be used in typical lead optimization stages of a drug discovery program. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit caveat ('under the right circumstances'), tempering the original unconditional claim while preserving the overall conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed wrmXpress, a unified framework for analyzing a variety of phenotypes matched to high-content experimental assays of free-living and parasitic nematodes and flatworms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have developed wrmXpress, a unified framework for analyzing a variety of phenotypes matched to high-content experimental assays of free-living and parasitic nematodes and flatworms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim and maintains the same contribution-level statement and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate its utility for analyzing a suite of phenotypes, including motility, development/size, and feeding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate its utility for analyzing a suite of phenotypes, including motility, development/size, fecundity, and feeding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same phenotypes but explicitly adds fecundity, clarifying and slightly broadening the set of reported phenotypes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WrmXpress can serve as an analytical workhorse for anthelmintic screening efforts across schistosomes, filarial nematodes, and free-living model nematodes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WrmXpress can serve as an analytical workhorse for anthelmintic screening efforts across schistosomes, filarial nematodes, and free-living model nematodes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result claim without substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WrmXpress holds promise for enabling collaboration among investigators with diverse interests. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: WrmXpress holds promise for enabling collaboration among investigators with diverse interests. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement is preserved verbatim in the publication with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep-learning framework CAMPA enables the learning of consistent molecular signatures across different cellular states and perturbations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAMPA is a deep learning framework that uses a conditional variational autoencoder to learn representations of molecular pixel profiles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more specific description of how CAMPA learns representations (CVAE and pixel profiles), clarifying the mechanism while preserving the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAMPA allows for quantitative comparisons of subcellular landmarks, including their sizes, shapes, molecular compositions, and relative spatial organization between conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clustering pixel-level representations identifies consistent subcellular landmarks that can be quantitatively compared. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the result in terms of clustering pixel-level representations but retains the claim that consistent subcellular landmarks can be quantitatively compared, a clarification of the method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using CAMPA, it was revealed that subnuclear organization changes in response to different perturbations of RNA production or processing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-resolution multiplexed immunofluorescence reveals how subcellular organization changes upon perturbation of RNA synthesis, RNA processing, or cell size. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the perturbation-driven changes in subcellular organization but adds cell size as an additional perturbation and emphasizes the imaging modality, broadening and clarifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study uncovers new links between the molecular composition of membraneless organelles and bulk RNA synthesis rates of single cells. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the specific claim about links between membraneless organelle composition and bulk RNA synthesis rates; instead it presents a different concluding capability, so the specific preprint claim appears removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CAMPA captures interpretable cellular phenotypes that may accelerate the systematic mapping of multiscale atlases of biological organization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds this higher-level concluding claim about CAMPA's broader utility, which did not appear in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed a method to accurately quantify sub-cellular pH in individual vesicles using fluorescent lifetime imaging microscopy (pHLIM). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: pHLIM can accurately quantify sub-cellular pH in individual vesicles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the core contribution that pHLIM accurately quantifies sub-cellular pH in individual vesicles with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: pHLIM exploits the linear pH dependent lifetime of the fluorescent protein mApple and uses deep learning models to automatically identify and measure the pH of subcellular compartments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's top-line claims omit the specific mechanism details (mApple lifetime linearity and deep learning segmentation) present in the preprint, so this mechanistic claim is not asserted in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have engineered mApple fusion proteins to measure the pH of the cytosol, endosomes, lysosomes and demonstrated the utility of pHLIM by measuring pH changes induced by drugs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: pHLIM can rapidly detect pH changes induced by drugs such as bafilomycin A1 and chloroquine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms drug-induced pH change detection (matching the demonstration) but does not enumerate the engineered fusion proteins or all compartments, narrowing and focusing the claimed result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: pHLIM is a simple and quantitative method to measure sub-cellular pH that has the potential to help with the design of the next generation of controlled drug release systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: pHLIM is a simple and quantitative method that will help to understand drug action and disease progression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the projected application from designing controlled drug release systems to a somewhat broader role in understanding drug action and disease progression, revising the implied downstream utility.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Polyethylenimine does not exhibit a proton sponge effect and had no measurable impact on the pH of endocytic vesicles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific experimental finding about polyethylenimine is present in the publication claims but was not stated in the preprint claims, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepVelo is a novel method that estimates the cell-specific dynamics of splicing kinetics using Graph Convolution Networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepVelo overcomes limitations of existing RNA velocity estimation methods by generalizing RNA velocity to cell populations with time-dependent kinetics and multiple lineages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the methodological advance (generalizing RNA velocity) but omits specific mention of GCNs and splicing kinetics in the top-line claim, clarifying the contribution at a higher level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepVelo generalizes RNA velocity to cell populations containing time-dependent kinetics and multiple lineages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepVelo overcomes limitations of existing RNA velocity estimation methods by generalizing RNA velocity to cell populations with time-dependent kinetics and multiple lineages. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This preprint claim is restated verbatim in the publication as a core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepVelo infers time-varying cellular rates of transcription, splicing, and degradation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepVelo infers time-varying cellular rates of transcription, splicing, and degradation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific result claim directly and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepVelo facilitates the study of more complex differentiation and lineage decision events in heterogeneous scRNA-seq data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Application of DeepVelo to various developmental and pathogenic processes demonstrates its capacity to study complex differentiation and lineage decision events in heterogeneous scRNA-seq data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication demonstrates applications and elevates the certainty of DeepVelo's capacity to study complex differentiation events, amplifying confidence relative to the preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepVelo is more computationally efficient than previous techniques. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's top-line claims do not assert computational efficiency, so this preprint claim about efficiency is not preserved in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification model achieved 0.97 validation accuracy and often >0.95 precision and recall for many classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification model achieved 0.97 validation accuracy and often > 0.95 precision and recall for many classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These performances were generally higher than 0.90 when tested on independent out-of-sample datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These performances were generally higher than 0.90 when tested on independent out-of-sample datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The out-of-sample performance claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a classification model trained to recognize 26 species or higher-level taxa. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a classification model trained to recognize 26 species or higher-level taxa that are common in Europe, with an emphasis on mammals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds geographic and taxonomic emphasis (common in Europe, emphasis on mammals), which clarifies and narrows the scope of the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We implemented our model in a software to classify images stored locally on a personal computer. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention a local-PC software implementation, so this specific implementation claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepFaune initiative is an ongoing project, allowing for the continuous addition of new species to the classification model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an initiative-level statement about ongoing expansion that was not present in the preprint claims, so this is an added publication claim.
